days.title: Week 1, Day 1: Numbers 0-4

words.example_sentence_target: ゼロは始まりの数字です。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zero wa hajimari no suuji desu.

words.example_sentence_target: これは一つです。
猫が二匹います。
りんごを三つください。
四月は春です。

days.cultural_note: The number four can be pronounced 'yon' or 'shi'. 'Shi' is sometimes avoided because it sounds like the word for death (死).
days.pronunciation_tip: Focus on short, crisp vowel sounds. 'Ichi' is 'ee-chee', not 'eye-chee'.

days.title: Week 1, Day 2: Numbers 5-9

words.example_sentence_target: 五時に会いましょう。
六つの卵を買いました。
七人の侍は有名な映画です。
words.example_sentence_native: Seven Samurai is a famous movie.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Shichinin no samurai wa yuumei na eiga desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 八は縁起の良い数字です。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Hachi wa engi no ii suuji desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 九月に日本へ行きます。
words.example_sentence_native: I will go to Japan in September.

days.cultural_note: Similar to 'shi', the pronunciation 'ku' for nine can be avoided as it sounds like the word for suffering (苦).
days.pronunciation_tip: 'Roku' has a light 'r' sound, almost like a cross between 'r' and 'l' in English.

days.title: Week 1, Day 3: Number 10 & Review
days.title_native: 第一週、三日目：数字 10 と復習

words.example_sentence_target: 十分待ってください。

days.cultural_note: The number 10 (十) is a fundamental building block for larger numbers in Japanese.
days.pronunciation_tip: 'Juu' is a long vowel sound, like 'joo'.

days.title: Week 1, Day 4: Numbers 11-15

words.example_sentence_target: 十一歳です。
十二月は冬です。
そのシャツは十三ドルです。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Sono shatsu wa juu-san doru desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 彼は十四歳です。
十五分後に会いましょう。

days.cultural_note: Japanese numbers from 11-19 are very logical: it's simply 'ten' plus the number. So, 11 is 'ten-one' (juu-ichi).
days.pronunciation_tip: Maintain the rhythm. Each syllable gets roughly equal stress: juu-i-chi.

days.title: Week 1, Day 5: Numbers 16-20

words.example_sentence_target: 十六ページを開けてください。
十七時（午後五時）に閉店します。
このビルは十八階建てです。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kono biru wa juu-hakkai date desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 十九世紀の芸術が好きです。
words.example_sentence_native: I like art from the 19th century.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Juu-kyuu-seiki no geijutsu ga suki desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 二十歳になりました。

days.cultural_note: For 20 years old, Japanese people often say 'hatachi' instead of 'ni-juu-sai'. It's a special term for this age.
days.pronunciation_tip: The pattern for 'twenty' is 'two-ten' (ni-juu). This pattern continues for 30, 40, etc.

days.title: Week 1, Day 6: Review 0-20
days.cultural_note: Practice counting aloud to build muscle memory for the number sounds.
days.pronunciation_tip: Listen to the audio for each number again and try to mimic the pronunciation and rhythm.

days.title: Week 1, Day 7: Counting Practice
days.title_native: 第一週、七日目：数える練習
days.cultural_note: In Japan, items are often counted using specific 'counter words'. For now, just focus on the numbers themselves.
days.pronunciation_tip: Try counting backwards from 20 to 0. This can be a great way to solidify your knowledge.
